name: field-group
description: Form field wrapper combining label, input slot, description, and error message, with a normalized validation contract (§7.1): states invalid | validating | disabled, a standardized required marker, and CSS-driven error visibility (no JS class toggling)

changes.note: BREAKING: validation state vocabulary normalized to invalid | validating | disabled — the shipped data-state="error" is renamed to data-state="invalid" to align with the framework's naming; new validating and disabled states added. The required marker is standardized as a [data-part="required"] element inside the label (the legacy [data-required] attribute still renders a marker for backward compatibility). Migration: replace data-state="error" with data-state="invalid" on every field-group (faqir upgrade three-way merges the CSS; page markup using the old state must be updated by hand — the field-wiring audit rule accepts both names during the transition).

required.description: Standardized required marker (§7.1). Placed inside the label, e.g. *; carries the marker color and is announced/hidden with the label. Canonical alternative to the legacy [data-required] attribute.

input.description: Slot for the input element (input, select, textarea, etc.)

error.description: Error message shown in error state

invalid.description: Constraint failed: destructive border, label recolored, and the [data-part="error"] message revealed by CSS (no JS). Control must carry aria-invalid="true" (field-wiring audit). Renamed from the pre-2.0.0 "error" state.

validating.description: Async constraint in flight (e.g. server-side uniqueness): neutral muted border, error not shown yet. Pair with aria-busy="true" on the control.

disabled.description: Whole group non-interactive: dimmed via --field-disabled-opacity, not-allowed cursor. The control itself is also [disabled].

required_attrs: label must have for attribute matching input id
aria-describedby on input pointing to description/error id
aria-invalid="true" on input in error state

templates.html: {description}
templates.html_with_error: {error}
